description: CIRIS Agent localization - complete coverage of all LLM-facing text, setup wizard, agent messages, DMA prompts, formatters, handlers, errors, and Discord strings
generation_note: Auto-generated translations. Native speaker review recommended before production use. v2.0 expanded en.json to cover all LLM-facing text including DMA prompts, system formatters, handler messages, escalation guidance, crisis resources, error messages, and Discord field labels. Non-English files cover setup/agent/status sections; prompts/handlers/errors/discord sections pending translation.

string_categories.prompts.dma: DMA system prompt text sent to LLM (PDMA, CSDMA, IDMA, DSDMA, ASPDMA, TSASPDMA)
string_categories.prompts.formatters: System snapshot, identity, user context headers sent to LLM
string_categories.prompts.escalation: Escalation stage guidance sent to LLM
string_categories.prompts.crisis: Crisis resource guidance text sent to LLM
string_categories.prompts.engine_overview: Engine overview text sent to LLM
string_categories.handlers: Handler follow-up thought messages injected into LLM context
string_categories.errors: API and service error messages shown to users
string_categories.discord: Discord embed field labels and deferral messages
string_categories.adapters.wallet: Wallet adapter tool descriptions, documentation, error messages (send_money, request_money, get_statement)
string_categories.adapters.navigation: Navigation adapter tool descriptions (geocode, reverse geocode, route calculation)
string_categories.adapters.weather: Weather adapter tool descriptions (current, forecast, alerts) and disclaimers
string_categories.mobile: Kotlin Multiplatform mobile UI strings (setup wizard, interaction screen, action labels, validation errors)

coverage_analysis.methodology: L1+L2 speaker totals from Ethnologue/UNESCO, deduplicated for multilingual overlap (~20%). Need weighting uses inverse income + inverse digital access (scale 0.5-5.0).

localization_roadmap.rationale: Ranked by composite score: speaker count, need weight (inverse income + inverse digital access), and overlap with existing languages. Mission alignment per Meta-Goal M-1: prioritize populations most underserved by current AI ethical frameworks.

tier_1_high_impact.name: Indonesian
tier_1_high_impact.rationale: World's 4th largest country, low overlap with existing languages, lower-middle income, growing digital population

tier_1_high_impact.name: Hausa
tier_1_high_impact.rationale: West Africa's largest lingua franca (Nigeria, Niger, Ghana), very low income, very low digital access, zero current AI ethical framework coverage

tier_1_high_impact.name: Persian
tier_1_high_impact.rationale: Covers Iran + Afghanistan (Dari) + Tajikistan (Tajik); Afghanistan speakers among world's most underserved

tier_2_medium_impact.name: Vietnamese
tier_2_medium_impact.rationale: 100M population, lower-middle income, rapidly growing digital access, no overlap with existing languages

tier_2_medium_impact.name: Punjabi
tier_2_medium_impact.rationale: Large speaker base but high overlap with Hindi/Urdu; fills gap for Gurmukhi-script speakers in Indian Punjab

tier_2_medium_impact.name: Telugu
tier_2_medium_impact.rationale: 4th largest language in India, distinct Dravidian script, lower-middle income rural base

tier_2_medium_impact.name: Tamil
tier_2_medium_impact.rationale: Major Dravidian language across India, Sri Lanka, Singapore, Malaysia; deep literary tradition

tier_3_completeness.name: Yoruba
tier_3_completeness.rationale: Nigeria's second major lingua franca; very high need, zero AI ethical coverage

tier_3_completeness.name: Burmese
tier_3_completeness.native_name: မြန်မာဘာသာ
tier_3_completeness.rationale: Myanmar, extremely underserved, very low digital access
